stem: Hypercalcaemia is found in
explanation: Hypercalcaemia causes: hyperparathyroidism, malignancy, vitamin D intoxication, sarcoidosis, thiazides, immobilisation.

statements.text: malabsorption
statements.explanation: Malabsorption causes vitamin D and calcium DEFICIENCY → hypocalcaemia.

statements.text: vitamin D intoxication
statements.explanation: Vitamin D intoxication increases intestinal Ca absorption → hypercalcaemia.

statements.text: primary hyperparathyroidism
statements.explanation: Primary hyperparathyroidism (high PTH) → hypercalcaemia.

statements.text: calcitonin deficiency
statements.explanation: Calcitonin deficiency does not typically cause hypercalcaemia (calcitonin role limited).

statements.text: vitamin D deficiency
statements.explanation: Vitamin D deficiency causes HYPOcalcaemia.

stem: Clinical signs of diabetic autonomic neuropathy include
explanation: Diabetic autonomic neuropathy: silent ischaemia, resting tachycardia, orthostatic hypotension, gastroparesis, anhidrosis.

statements.text: painless myocardial ischaemia
statements.explanation: Silent MI is a hallmark of diabetic autonomic neuropathy.

statements.text: gastroparesis
statements.explanation: Gastroparesis is common.

statements.text: resting tachycardia
statements.explanation: Resting tachycardia from vagal denervation.

statements.text: increased sweating
statements.explanation: Diabetic neuropathy typically causes HYPOhidrosis (anhidrosis), not increased sweating.

statements.text: orthostatic hypotension
statements.explanation: Orthostatic hypotension is a classic feature.

stem: Spread of a spinal dose of local anaesthetic in adults is increased with
explanation: Factors increasing spinal block spread: increasing age, pregnancy, obesity, hyperbaric solution + position, larger volume/dose, increased intra-abdominal pressure.

statements.text: cephalad orientation of the needle aperture
statements.explanation: Cephalad needle aperture orientation increases cranial spread.

statements.text: increasing age
statements.explanation: Older patients have smaller CSF volume → greater spread.

statements.text: increasing height
statements.explanation: Taller patients with the same dose may have more variable spread; classically increased spread is taught for certain populations.

statements.text: high body mass index
statements.explanation: Higher BMI compresses dural sac → greater spread.

statements.text: pregnancy
statements.explanation: Pregnancy reduces epidural/CSF volume → increased spread.

stem: Regarding caudal anaesthesia
explanation: Caudal anaesthesia: identify sacral hiatus and PSIS landmarks; prone or lateral position; safer in children due to anatomy; saline test for misplacement.

statements.text: the anterior superior iliac spines can be used as a landmark
statements.explanation: Posterior superior iliac spines (PSIS) are landmarks for caudal block, not anterior.

statements.text: the prone position can be used when administering caudal anaesthesia
statements.explanation: Prone position is a recognised technique for caudal anaesthesia.

statements.text: spread of local anaesthetic is greater in adults than in children
statements.explanation: Spread in adults is LESS than in children (smaller volume needed per kg in children, but reach is more reliable in children).

statements.text: systemic absorption of local anaesthetic is greater than from a lumbar epidural
statements.explanation: Caudal absorption is greater due to vascular sacral epidural space.

statements.text: subcutaneous needle placement can be detected using saline injection
statements.explanation: Saline injection swelling detects subcutaneous misplacement.

stem: Disadvantages of supraglottic jet ventilation include
explanation: Jet ventilation: disadvantages include barotrauma, debris distribution, vocal cord movement, difficult CO2 monitoring; suited to short laryngeal surgery.

statements.text: considerable vibration and movement of the vocal cords
statements.explanation: Vocal cord vibration is a recognised disadvantage.

statements.text: it is impossible to monitor end-tidal carbon dioxide concentration
statements.explanation: ETCO2 can be approximated with side-stream sampling.

statements.text: it obstructs the view for the surgeon
statements.explanation: Supraglottic jet ventilation generally maintains surgical field view; it doesn’t obstruct.

statements.text: blood, smoke and debris may be blown into the distal trachea
statements.explanation: Blood and debris can be blown distally.

statements.text: it is impossible to use high-frequency ventilation
statements.explanation: High-frequency jet ventilation IS possible — feature not disadvantage.

stem: In the anesthetic management of tetralogy of Fallot, the following are included:
explanation: ToF anaesthesia: keep SVR high, avoid tachycardia and PVR rise, treat hypercyanotic spells immediately.

statements.text: a reduction in systemic vascular resistance (SVR)
statements.explanation: Reduction in SVR worsens R→L shunt in ToF — must MAINTAIN SVR.

statements.text: ventilatory measures to increase pulmonary vascular resistance (PVR)
statements.explanation: Avoid manoeuvres that lower PVR (paradoxical); some maintain PVR.

statements.text: avoiding tachycardia
statements.explanation: Tachycardia exacerbates dynamic RVOT obstruction.

statements.text: avoiding a reduction in the pulmonary vascular resistance to systemic vascular resistance ratio (PVR/SVR)
statements.explanation: Maintaining favourable PVR/SVR ratio is the principle.

statements.text: immediate treatment of hypercyanosis
statements.explanation: Hypercyanotic spells require immediate treatment (phenylephrine, β-blocker, fluid).

stem: In the evaluation of an adult patient for lung resection due to carcinoma:
explanation: Lung resection assessment: FEV1, ppoFEV1, DLCO, VO2max < 10 → very high risk; pack-years correlate with complications.

statements.text: preoperatively, a forced vital capacity (FVC) of 60% of predicted is acceptable for pneumonectomy
statements.explanation: 60% FVC is too low for pneumonectomy (need higher reserves).

statements.text: patients with a maximum oxygen consumption below 10 ml/kg/min have a very high risk of operative mortality
statements.explanation: VO2max <10 mL/kg/min indicates very high operative mortality.

statements.text: the higher the number of pack-years of cigarette smoking, the greater the risk of postoperative pulmonary complications
statements.explanation: Pack-years correlate with postoperative pulmonary complications.

statements.text: resting arterial PaO2 is a good predictor of risk
statements.explanation: Resting PaO2 has predictive value (low PaO2 = higher risk).

stem: Among the causes for observing an arterial blood gas analysis with a base excess of -12 and an HCO3- of 16 mmol/L, the following are included:
explanation: Metabolic acidosis (BE −12): lactic (sepsis, hypoperfusion), ketoacidosis, renal failure, diarrhoea. Opioids cause respiratory acidosis.

statements.text: morphine overdose
statements.explanation: Opioid OD → RESPIRATORY acidosis (CO2 retention), not metabolic.

statements.text: acute renal tubular necrosis
statements.explanation: ATN can cause uraemic metabolic acidosis.

statements.text: prolonged diarrhea and vomiting
statements.explanation: Diarrhoea causes loss of bicarbonate (vomiting causes alkalosis); statement combines both — net acidosis is possible from diarrhoea dominance.

statements.text: severe sepsis
statements.explanation: Sepsis → lactic acidosis.

statements.text: period following cardiopulmonary resuscitation
statements.explanation: Post-CPR → lactic acidosis from tissue hypoperfusion.

stem: In the diagnosis of brain death:
explanation: Brain death diagnosis: exclude confounders (drugs, hypothermia, metabolic), demonstrate absent brainstem reflexes + apnoea test. Spinal reflexes may persist.

statements.text: the caloric or temperature test is used to assess the integrity of the fifth (5th) cranial nerve
statements.explanation: Caloric test assesses vestibulocochlear (VIII) and oculomotor brainstem reflexes, not the 5th nerve.

statements.text: an isoelectric electroencephalogram (EEG) confirms brainstem death
statements.explanation: Isoelectric EEG is supportive but NOT REQUIRED for brainstem death diagnosis.

statements.text: reflex leg movements may still be observed
statements.explanation: Spinal reflexes (limb movements) may persist after brain death.

statements.text: the absence of neuromuscular blockade must be confirmed
statements.explanation: Confounding factors including NMB must be excluded.

statements.text: in a patient with hypothermia, clinical criteria are not valid
statements.explanation: Hypothermia invalidates clinical brain death testing.

stem: Acute pancreatitis is associated with:
explanation: Acute pancreatitis: hypocalcaemia, hyperlipidaemia, ileus, ARDS/hypoxaemia, DIC, AKI; severity scored by Ranson/APACHE-II/BISAP.

statements.text: hypocalcemia
statements.explanation: Hypocalcaemia is common (fat saponification).

statements.text: hypolipidemia
statements.explanation: Hyperlipidaemia (not hypo) is associated with acute pancreatitis.

statements.text: paralytic ileus
statements.explanation: Paralytic ileus is common.

statements.text: hypoxemia
statements.explanation: Hypoxaemia from ARDS or pleural effusion.

statements.text: disseminated intravascular coagulation
statements.explanation: DIC is a recognised complication.

stem: In a ventilated patient with hypotension, among the rapid diagnostic tests for pulmonary embolism, the following are included:
explanation: Massive PE in ventilated hypotensive patient: bedside TTE for RV strain, CTPA when stable. D-dimer not diagnostic at point of care.

statements.text: D-dimer assay
statements.explanation: D-dimer has poor positive predictive value; cannot guide bedside diagnosis in ventilated patients.

statements.text: bedside transthoracic echocardiography
statements.explanation: Bedside TTE shows RV strain in massive PE.

statements.text: right heart catheterization and monitoring of pulmonary artery pressure
statements.explanation: Right heart catheterisation is invasive and not "rapid" diagnostic.

statements.text: pulmonary scintigraphy (ventilation-perfusion lung scan with radionuclides)
statements.explanation: V/Q scan is not practical in unstable ventilated patients.

statements.text: spiral computed tomography with contrast
statements.explanation: CTPA (spiral CT with contrast) is the gold standard if stable for transport.

stem: In pregnant women who present with:
explanation: Antepartum haemorrhage: abruption may be concealed → DIC risk; placenta accreta needs elective CS; spinal contraindicated with active bleeding.

statements.text: abruptio placentae, bleeding may be controlled or confined
statements.explanation: Concealed (occult) abruption is recognised in placental abruption.

statements.text: placenta accreta, vaginal delivery is preferred
statements.explanation: Placenta accreta requires elective caesarean with multidisciplinary team — vaginal delivery contraindicated.

statements.text: active bleeding due to premature placental abruption, general anesthesia is preferred
statements.explanation: GA is reserved for haemodynamically unstable cases; spinal/CSE is otherwise preferred.

statements.text: abruptio placentae, initial clinical findings may include abdominal pain and fetal distress
statements.explanation: Abruption presents with abdominal pain and fetal distress.

statements.text: complete placenta previa, spinal anesthesia (subarachnoid block) for cesarean section is contraindicated
statements.explanation: Complete placenta previa: spinal/neuraxial is generally contraindicated due to bleeding risk.

stem: Appropriate treatment for wide-complex tachycardia occurring during anesthesia includes the administration of:
explanation: Wide-complex tachycardia (assume VT until proven otherwise): amiodarone, lidocaine, procainamide, electrical cardioversion if unstable.

statements.text: amiodarone
statements.explanation: Amiodarone is first-line for wide-complex tachycardia of unclear origin.

statements.text: adenosine
statements.explanation: Adenosine is for narrow-complex SVT only; risky in wide-complex.

statements.text: verapamil
statements.explanation: Verapamil is contraindicated in wide-complex tachycardia (may worsen VT).

statements.text: calcium gluconate
statements.explanation: Calcium gluconate is not standard for tachyarrhythmias.

statements.text: digoxin
statements.explanation: Digoxin is not first-line for acute wide-complex tachycardia.

stem: Complex regional pain syndrome (CRPS) in the mother is associated with:
explanation: CRPS: spontaneous regional pain, allodynia/hyperalgesia, vasomotor and sudomotor changes; often follows minor injury (e.g. wrist fracture).

statements.text: wrist fracture
statements.explanation: Wrist fracture is a classic precipitant of CRPS type 1.

statements.text: cold skin
statements.explanation: Cold, mottled skin is a vasomotor feature.

statements.text: excessive sensitivity to deep palpation
statements.explanation: Hyperalgesia to deep palpation is characteristic.

statements.text: abnormal sensitivity to light skin stimulation
statements.explanation: Allodynia (pain from non-painful stimuli) is a hallmark.

statements.text: muscle hypotonia

stem: A plasma sodium level of 121 mmol/L is associated with:
explanation: Hyponatraemia (~121): SIADH (small-cell ca, head injury, drugs), cerebral salt wasting (SAH), psychogenic polydipsia, Addison’s disease.

statements.text: syndrome of inappropriate antidiuretic hormone secretion (SIADH)
statements.explanation: SIADH causes dilutional hyponatraemia.

statements.text: small cell lung carcinoma
statements.explanation: Small-cell lung cancer is a paraneoplastic cause of SIADH.

statements.text: Cushing’s syndrome
statements.explanation: Cushing causes hypernatraemia / fluid retention, not hyponatraemia.

statements.text: acute subarachnoid hemorrhage
statements.explanation: SAH commonly causes cerebral salt wasting → hyponatraemia.

statements.text: diabetes insipidus
statements.explanation: Diabetes insipidus causes HYPERnatraemia.

stem: Regarding anesthesia for outpatient cardioversion:
explanation: Outpatient cardioversion: short GA with propofol; no routine intubation; assume full stomach if not fasted.

statements.text: short-duration anesthesia is necessary because the procedure is painful and distressing
statements.explanation: Short-duration GA is needed because cardioversion is painful.

statements.text: midazolam is preferred over propofol
statements.explanation: Propofol is preferred over midazolam (better titration and depth).

statements.text: elective cases are generally performed with general anesthesia and endotracheal intubation
statements.explanation: Most elective cardioversions are GA without intubation — short and uncomplicated.

statements.text: in patients with a “full stomach,” intravenous anesthesia without endotracheal intubation is preferred
statements.explanation: Full-stomach patients need rapid sequence with intubation; OR delay procedure.

statements.text: barbiturates are contraindicated
statements.explanation: Barbiturates are not contraindicated but propofol is preferred.

stem: In patients with adult respiratory distress syndrome and heterogeneous lung mechanics:
explanation: ARDS ventilation: low tidal volume (6 mL/kg), adequate PEEP, recruitment manoeuvres, plateau pressure <30 cmH2O.

statements.text: positive end-expiratory pressure (PEEP) prevents alveolar derecruitment
statements.explanation: PEEP holds alveoli open, preventing derecruitment.

statements.text: inspiratory pauses up to 40 cm H2O and lasting up to 2 minutes are generally recommended
statements.explanation: Sustained inspiratory holds of 30–40 cmH2O are recommended for RECRUITMENT for 30 seconds, not 2 minutes — statement is incorrect.

statements.text: applying PEEP may improve ventilation/perfusion matching
statements.explanation: PEEP improves V/Q matching by recruitment.

statements.text: regional lung overdistension can be avoided by using normal tidal volumes
statements.explanation: Low (6 mL/kg) tidal volumes — not "normal" — minimise overdistension.

statements.text: positive pressure breaths are preferentially distributed to regions of normal compliance
statements.explanation: In heterogeneous lungs, positive pressure preferentially ventilates compliant regions (overdistension risk).

stem: In a patient with subarachnoid anesthesia up to T4, the inability to cough forcefully is due to:
explanation: High spinal: abdominal and intercostal muscles paralysed → impaired cough. Diaphragm (C3–5) preserved until cervical block.

statements.text: diaphragm paralysis
statements.explanation: T4 spinal does not affect diaphragm (C3–C5).

statements.text: paralysis of abdominal muscles
statements.explanation: Abdominal muscles (T6–T12) needed for forceful cough; paralysis impairs cough.

statements.text: paralysis of intercostal muscles
statements.explanation: Intercostal muscles (T2–T12) needed for cough; paralysed.

statements.text: reduction in functional residual capacity (FRC)
statements.explanation: FRC falls due to abdominal compression release in supine.

statements.text: blockade of the pulmonary stretch reflex
statements.explanation: Pulmonary stretch reflex blockade is not the cough mechanism.

stem: Severe, intermittent pain in the lower face, triggered by touching the lower jaw:
explanation: Trigeminal neuralgia (CN V): first-line carbamazepine, MRI to exclude secondary cause, refractory → MVD or RF ablation; opioids ineffective.

statements.text: may require radiofrequency ablation of the Gasserian ganglion
statements.explanation: Refractory cases need RF ablation of Gasserian ganglion or microvascular decompression.

statements.text: can be treated with carbamazepine
statements.explanation: Carbamazepine is first-line.

statements.text: is due to activation of the seventh cranial nerve
statements.explanation: Trigeminal neuralgia involves the 5th cranial nerve, not the 7th.

statements.text: requires investigation with MRI (magnetic resonance imaging)
statements.explanation: MRI investigates for secondary causes.

statements.text: responds well to treatment with opioids
statements.explanation: Trigeminal neuralgia responds POORLY to opioids.

stem: Regarding cystic fibrosis:
explanation: Cystic fibrosis: autosomal recessive CFTR mutation; exocrine gland dysfunction; lungs (recurrent infection), pancreas (insufficiency), sweat glands.

statements.text: leads to a reduction in residual volume and vital capacity
statements.explanation: CF: airway obstruction → increased RV but decreased VC; both depend on stage; in advanced disease RV increases.

statements.text: is associated with dehydration and electrolyte imbalances
statements.explanation: Sweat Cl- loss → dehydration and electrolyte imbalance.

statements.text: increases ciliary activity
statements.explanation: Ciliary activity is REDUCED (thick mucus) — not increased.

statements.text: primarily affects the pulmonary and gastrointestinal systems
statements.explanation: CF primarily affects lungs and pancreas/GI tract.

statements.text: is a hereditary disease of the endocrine glands
statements.explanation: CF is hereditary disease of EXOCRINE glands (CFTR), not endocrine.

stem: Among the recommendations for preoperative fasting:
explanation: ESA fasting: 6 h solids, 4 h breast milk, 2 h clear fluids; modify (longer) for delayed gastric emptying.

statements.text: in newborns, breast milk is allowed up to 4 hours before surgery
statements.explanation: Breast milk 4 h fast in neonates (paediatric ESA).

statements.text: a 2-hour fasting period for clear liquids applies to all patients
statements.explanation: Clear fluids 2 h fast for all (modern fasting).

statements.text: surgery should be postponed in patients who have chewed gum immediately before anesthesia induction
statements.explanation: Chewing gum doesn’t require postponement under modern evidence.

statements.text: the volume of liquid ingested is more important than the type of liquid ingested
statements.explanation: Volume of liquid is less important than type; mostly type matters but volume also limited; this statement is debatable. Conservative TRUE.

statements.text: patients with delayed gastric emptying may follow the same guidelines as healthy adults
statements.explanation: Delayed gastric emptying requires LONGER fasting than healthy adults.

stem: The Mallampati score depends on the visibility of:
explanation: Mallampati classification: visibility of soft palate, uvula, and tonsillar pillars predicts laryngoscopic view; not the hard palate.

statements.text: the uvula
statements.explanation: Mallampati assesses uvula visibility.

statements.text: the soft palate
statements.explanation: Soft palate visibility differentiates classes.

statements.text: the base of the tongue
statements.explanation: Tongue base is more about ULBT (upper lip bite test) than Mallampati.

statements.text: the pharynx (“throat”)
statements.explanation: Pharynx visibility is part of Mallampati.

statements.text: the hard palate
statements.explanation: Hard palate is not assessed by Mallampati.

stem: Among the adverse effects of intraoperative hypothermia:
explanation: Perioperative hypothermia: coagulopathy, infection, prolonged drug effect, cardiac complications, longer PACU stay.

statements.text: prolonged motor blockade after spinal anesthesia
statements.explanation: Hypothermia prolongs LA effect → longer motor block.

statements.text: increased risk of surgical site infection
statements.explanation: Increased SSI risk from vasoconstriction and immune impairment.

statements.text: prolonged stay in the postanesthesia care unit
statements.explanation: Longer PACU stay.

statements.text: coagulopathy
statements.explanation: Coagulopathy from temperature-dependent enzymes.

statements.text: resistance to depolarizing muscle relaxants
statements.explanation: Hypothermia POTENTIATES (not resists) muscle relaxants.

stem: Severe diabetic ketoacidosis is associated with:
explanation: DKA: high anion-gap metabolic acidosis, hyperglycaemia, ketosis, dehydration, total-body K+ depletion; treat insulin + fluids + K+ replacement.

statements.text: decreased intracellular potassium concentration
statements.explanation: DKA has total-body K+ DEPLETION but plasma K+ often normal or HIGH (acidosis-driven shift); intracellular K+ falls.

statements.text: a normal anion gap
statements.explanation: DKA has HIGH anion gap, not normal.

statements.text: total body dehydration
statements.explanation: Osmotic diuresis causes severe dehydration.

statements.text: plasma hyperosmolarity
statements.explanation: Hyperglycaemia raises plasma osmolarity.

statements.text: hypoventilation
statements.explanation: Kussmaul breathing is HYPERventilation; "hypoventilation" is wrong if literal — but statement implies hypoventilation which is FALSE.

stem: In a bedridden patient with hemiparesis and leftward gaze deviation:
explanation: Acute stroke: time-critical; thrombolysis window 4.5 h, thrombectomy up to 24 h in selected cases. Imaging guides decision.

statements.text: spontaneous intracranial hemorrhage is the most likely diagnosis
statements.explanation: Ischaemic stroke is more common (~85%) than haemorrhagic.

statements.text: intravenous thrombolysis with tissue plasminogen activator should be initiated within 2 hours of ischemic stroke
statements.explanation: Thrombolysis window is up to 4.5 hours, not 2.

statements.text: ischemic cortical lesions are generally observable within the first hour after the onset of clinical signs
statements.explanation: CT changes are often delayed several hours; MRI DWI shows changes early.

statements.text: a left internal capsule infarct is the most likely diagnosis
statements.explanation: Internal capsule infarct can cause pure motor stroke with hemiparesis.

statements.text: a right thalamic infarct is a …
statements.explanation: Left gaze deviation suggests right cortical/cerebral hemisphere lesion (eyes look TOWARDS lesion in cortical infarct); left thalamic infarct could explain — statement of "right thalamic" is incomplete in source.

stem: In an obstetric emergency due to hemorrhage:
explanation: Obstetric haemorrhage: O Rh- RBCs immediately, balanced product transfusion, cell salvage acceptable, target platelets >50 and fibrinogen >2 g/L.

statements.text: ABO-specific red blood cells should be administered as soon as possible
statements.explanation: In life-threatening obstetric haemorrhage, group O Rh- is given immediately, not waiting for ABO matching.

statements.text: intraoperative red blood cell salvage and autotransfusion are contraindicated due to amniotic fluid contamination
statements.explanation: Cell salvage is now acceptable in obstetric haemorrhage with leucocyte filters.

statements.text: group O fresh frozen plasma is suitable
statements.explanation: Group O FFP (or AB FFP, universal donor) acceptable.

statements.text: group O Rh D negative red blood cells are transfused first
statements.explanation: Group O Rh- RBCs are the universal donor for emergencies.

statements.text: platelet count should be maintained above 100,000 per mm³
statements.explanation: Platelet count target >50 in active bleeding; >100 in CNS/eye — >100 isn’t routine.

stem: An acute pulmonary embolism is associated with:
explanation: PE: increased dead space, raised PAP and RV strain, low CO with low SvO2, normal PCWP. CT pulmonary angio confirms diagnosis.

statements.text: a decrease in the dead space to tidal volume ratio (VD/VT)
statements.explanation: PE INCREASES dead-space ratio (perfusion deficit) — not decrease.

statements.text: decreased right atrial pressure
statements.explanation: Right atrial pressure rises with RV strain.

statements.text: increased mean pulmonary artery pressure
statements.explanation: Mean PAP rises in massive PE.

statements.text: elevated mixed venous oxygen saturation
statements.explanation: Mixed venous O2 sat FALLS (low CO, high extraction).

statements.text: increased pulmonary capillary pressure
statements.explanation: PCWP is normal or low in PE (left heart unaffected).

stem: In myasthenia gravis:
explanation: Myasthenia gravis: post-synaptic AChR antibodies; treat with pyridostigmine, immunosuppression, thymectomy, IVIG/plasmapheresis for crisis.

statements.text: antibodies against acetylcholine receptors are often present at the skeletal muscle level
statements.explanation: Anti-AChR antibodies in 80–85% of generalised MG; muscle-specific kinase (MuSK) in others.

statements.text: there is chronic weakness of respiratory muscles
statements.explanation: Chronic respiratory muscle weakness, especially in myasthenic crisis.

statements.text: acetylcholinesterase inhibitors are administered orally
statements.explanation: Pyridostigmine taken orally is first-line treatment.

statements.text: thymectomy may improve symptoms
statements.explanation: Thymectomy improves symptoms in selected patients.

statements.text: drugs that may trigger malignant hyperthermia are contraindicated
statements.explanation: MH triggers (volatiles, sux) are not specifically contraindicated in MG — but caution; this statement may be more about MG drug interactions. Conservative TRUE.

stem: Adrenocortical insufficiency causes:
explanation: Adrenal insufficiency (Addison’s): hypotension, hyperkalaemia, hyponatraemia, hypoglycaemia, hyperpigmentation; treat with hydrocortisone + fluids.

statements.text: hypertension
statements.explanation: Addison’s causes HYPOtension, not hypertension.

statements.text: abdominal pain
statements.explanation: Abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting are common.

statements.text: hypokalemia
statements.explanation: HYPERkalaemia (low aldosterone), not hypokalaemia.

statements.text: bradycardia
statements.explanation: Bradycardia is not typical (tachycardia from hypovolaemia).

statements.text: pigmentation
statements.explanation: Hyperpigmentation (high ACTH) is classic in primary adrenal insufficiency.

stem: Pyloric stenosis is associated with:
explanation: Pyloric stenosis: hypochloraemic, hypokalaemic metabolic alkalosis with paradoxical aciduria; correct fluid and electrolytes before surgery.

statements.text: metabolic acidosis
statements.explanation: Pyloric stenosis causes ALKALOSIS (loss of HCl), not acidosis.

statements.text: uremia
statements.explanation: Uraemia is not characteristic.

statements.text: hyponatremia
statements.explanation: Hyponatraemia from vomiting losses.

statements.text: hypokalemia
statements.explanation: Hypokalaemia from gastric and renal losses.

statements.text: hypochloremia
statements.explanation: Hypochloraemia from loss of HCl in vomit.

stem: Among pediatric pathologies with a high risk of malignant hyperthermia:
explanation: MH triggers: volatiles + sux. Susceptibility associated with central core disease, multi-mini-core disease, King-Denborough syndrome. DMD has MH-like episodes.

statements.text: osteogenesis imperfecta
statements.explanation: Osteogenesis imperfecta is not associated with MH (although has anaesthetic concerns).

statements.text: Duchenne muscular dystrophy
statements.explanation: Duchenne muscular dystrophy is associated with MH-like reactions and sux-induced hyperkalaemia.

statements.text: cerebral palsy
statements.explanation: Cerebral palsy is not specifically MH-associated.

statements.text: Pierre Robin syndrome
statements.explanation: Pierre Robin is an airway concern, not MH.

statements.text: diabetes mellitus
statements.explanation: Diabetes is not MH-associated.

stem: Regarding propofol infusion syndrome:
explanation: PRIS: high-dose long-duration propofol → mitochondrial fatty-acid oxidation impairment, lactic acidosis, rhabdomyolysis, cardiac failure.

statements.text: it is associated with a genetically determined defect in the ryanodine receptor
statements.explanation: PRIS is mitochondrial dysfunction; RyR mutations cause MH, not PRIS.

statements.text: it is associated with liver failure
statements.explanation: PRIS commonly causes hepatic and cardiac failure.

statements.text: it is associated with a combination of lactic acidosis and rhabdomyolysis
statements.explanation: Lactic acidosis + rhabdomyolysis are characteristic.

statements.text: it occurs only in children with a genetic defect in lipid metabolism
statements.explanation: Occurs in both children and adults at high-dose, prolonged infusion; not "only in children with genetic defect".

statements.text: treatment includes dantrolene
statements.explanation: Dantrolene is for MH; PRIS treatment is supportive (stop infusion, dialysis).

stem: Known complications of celiac plexus block include:
explanation: Coeliac plexus block: hypotension, diarrhoea, back pain; rare paraplegia from spinal artery injection.

statements.text: hematuria
statements.explanation: Transient haematuria can occur.

statements.text: post-dural puncture headache
statements.explanation: PDPH is rare (retroperitoneal block) but reported.

statements.text: hypertension
statements.explanation: HYPOtension (sympathectomy) — not hypertension — is the classic complication.

statements.text: constipation
statements.explanation: Diarrhoea (sympathetic loss) — not constipation — is typical.

statements.text: urinary incontinence
statements.explanation: Urinary incontinence is not typical.

stem: Regarding pediatric anesthesia:
explanation: Paediatric basics: ETT ID (age/4)+4 (uncuffed); blood volume ~80 mL/kg neonate, ~70 mL/kg older child; TV 6–8 mL/kg.

statements.text: the normal dose of neostigmine is 0.04-0.08 mg/kg
statements.explanation: Neostigmine 0.04–0.08 mg/kg is the standard reversal dose.

statements.text: newborns are more sensitive to the effects of succinylcholine than older children
statements.explanation: Neonates have prolonged sux duration / different sensitivity — TRUE per some texts.

statements.text: in a 6-year-old child, the ideal internal diameter of the endotracheal tube is 4 mm
statements.explanation: ETT internal diameter for a 6-year-old ≈ (age/4) + 4 = 5.5 mm, not 4.

statements.text: in a 4 kg child, the blood volume is approximately 500 ml
statements.explanation: 4 kg child has ~80 mL/kg = 320 mL, not 500.

statements.text: the normal tidal volume for a 4 kg child is 20-25 ml
statements.explanation: Tidal volume ~6–8 mL/kg → 24–32 mL for 4 kg child; 20–25 is on the low end.

stem: Regarding tourniquets applied during orthopedic surgery:
explanation: Tourniquet use: pressure 100 mmHg above SBP (lower limb), max 90–120 min, apply after exsanguination; deflation causes physiological changes.

statements.text: deflation can cause clinically significant adverse effects in patients with compromised cardiorespiratory function
statements.explanation: Tourniquet deflation releases ischaemic metabolites → potential hypotension/arrhythmia.

statements.text: when applied to the lower limb, they should be inflated to a pressure of 50 mmHg above systolic arterial pressure
statements.explanation: Lower limb tourniquet pressure ~100 mmHg above SBP (not 50).

statements.text: in healthy patients, they should be deflated after a maximum of 2 hours of inflation
statements.explanation: Maximum tourniquet time generally 1.5–2 hours.

statements.text: they should be applied after limb exsanguination
statements.explanation: Apply tourniquet after limb exsanguination.

statements.text: they are absolutely contraindicated in patients with sickle cell trait
statements.explanation: Sickle cell trait isn’t an absolute contraindication; sickle cell DISEASE is relative contraindication.

stem: Among anesthetic considerations in burn victims:
explanation: Burn anaesthesia: fluid resuscitation, AVOID sux >24 h post-burn (hyperkalaemia), resistance to non-depolarisers; CO poisoning left-shifts ODC.

statements.text: colloids to address the initial decrease in intravascular fluid volume
statements.explanation: Colloids/crystalloids per modified Parkland formula address volume loss.

statements.text: increased requirements for non-depolarizing muscle relaxants
statements.explanation: Burns up-regulate nAChRs → resistance to non-depolarisers → higher doses needed.

statements.text: avoiding succinylcholine in the first 6 hours after burn injury
statements.explanation: Avoid sux from 24–48 h post-burn (not 6 h) due to extra-junctional receptor upregulation.

statements.text: in a smoke inhalation situation, a leftward shift of the oxyhemoglobin curve
statements.explanation: CO poisoning shifts curve LEFT impairing O2 release — statement of "leftward shift" is TRUE for CO; "leftward shift" is bad. Statement asks if it occurs as part of smoke inhalation. Mark FALSE only if statement reads as describing this incorrectly; the statement is TRUE in context — but with the way phrased ("a leftward shift" as anaesthetic consideration), it is a recognised finding; mark FALSE conservatively as the source is ambiguous.

statements.text: thiopental is contraindicated
statements.explanation: Thiopental can be used cautiously; not absolutely contraindicated.

stem: Regarding cardiac tamponade:
explanation: Cardiac tamponade: Beck’s triad, pulsus paradoxus, echo confirms; immediate pericardiocentesis.

statements.text: the patient may present with orthopnea
statements.explanation: Orthopnea from impaired venous return.

statements.text: it can be ruled out when the ECG is normal
statements.explanation: ECG often abnormal (low voltage, electrical alternans); cannot rule out.

statements.text: its diagnosis can be ruled out by a normal chest X-ray
statements.explanation: CXR may be normal acutely.

statements.text: it is treated with immediate pericardiocentesis
statements.explanation: Pericardiocentesis is life-saving emergency treatment.

statements.text: it may present with mesenteric ischemia
statements.explanation: Mesenteric ischaemia is not a typical tamponade presentation.

stem: A reduction in thoraco-pulmonary compliance is often associated with:
explanation: Reduced compliance: restrictive disease (fibrosis), pulmonary oedema, kyphoscoliosis, atelectasis. Increased compliance: emphysema.

statements.text: asthma
statements.explanation: Asthma has normal or increased compliance (air trapping); not reduced.

statements.text: left ventricular failure
statements.explanation: LV failure → pulmonary oedema → reduced compliance.

statements.text: kyphoscoliosis
statements.explanation: Kyphoscoliosis restricts chest wall → reduced compliance.

statements.text: emphysema
statements.explanation: Emphysema increases compliance (elastic recoil loss).

statements.text: pulmonary fibrosis
statements.explanation: Pulmonary fibrosis reduces compliance.

stem: Regarding outpatient surgery patients:
explanation: Day-case selection: ASA 1–2 (some stable 3), escorted home, encourage regional anaesthesia, modern fasting.

statements.text: they should not be proposed for regional anesthesia techniques
statements.explanation: Day-case patients can have regional anaesthesia (encouraged).

statements.text: they should avoid alcoholic beverages for the first 24 hours post-surgery
statements.explanation: Avoiding alcohol 24 h post-op is appropriate.

statements.text: they should not drink any liquids for 6 hours before anesthesia
statements.explanation: Modern fasting allows clear fluids up to 2 h — not 6 h.

statements.text: they should be able to return home alone after surgery
statements.explanation: Day-case patients must have responsible adult escort home.

statements.text: they are usually ASA 1 or ASA 2
statements.explanation: ASA 1–2 typical for day-case selection.

stem: In a patient with an unstable chest, ventilated, with a chest drain outputting air at a rate of 1.5 L/min, appropriate therapeutic measures include:
explanation: Bronchopleural fistula on PPV: minimise mean airway pressure, low PEEP, consider HFOV or lung isolation; never clamp the drain.

statements.text: reducing peak inspiratory pressure
statements.explanation: Reducing peak inspiratory pressure reduces fistula flow.

statements.text: reducing the I:E ratio
statements.explanation: Reducing inspiratory time / I:E ratio reduces leak.

statements.text: decreasing respiratory rate
statements.explanation: Lower RR reduces minute leak.

statements.text: adding 10 cm H2O of positive end-expiratory pressure (PEEP)
statements.explanation: PEEP INCREASES fistula leak — counterproductive.

statements.text: clamping the chest drain
statements.explanation: Clamping chest drain risks tension — never appropriate.

stem: Regarding inotropic support in critically ill patients:
explanation: Inotrope mechanism: cAMP/Ca2+ pathways. CHF down-regulates β-receptors. Septic shock: NA ± dobutamine; PDE inhibitors raise HR.

statements.text: in chronic heart failure, the effect of beta-adrenergic drugs is reduced
statements.explanation: CHF down-regulates β-receptors → reduced β-agonist response.

statements.text: in septic shock, a combination of norepinephrine and dobutamine is recommended
statements.explanation: Septic shock: NA + dobutamine combination is recommended.

statements.text: the predominant effect of dobutamine on blood vessels is vasoconstriction
statements.explanation: Dobutamine causes vasodilation predominantly (β2), not vasoconstriction.

statements.text: phosphodiesterase 3 (PDE3) inhibitors have no influence on heart rate
statements.explanation: PDE3 inhibitors (milrinone) increase heart rate.

statements.text: most drugs act by increasing the cytosolic Ca2+ concentration in cardiomyocytes
statements.explanation: Most inotropes act by raising cytosolic Ca2+.

stem: Regarding invasive arterial pressure measurement:
explanation: Invasive arterial monitoring: beat-to-beat BP, waveform analysis, blood sampling; complications low with appropriate technique.

statements.text: analysis of the arterial waveform can provide additional diagnostic information
statements.explanation: Arterial waveform analysis gives info on volume status, CO, and arrhythmias.

statements.text: it should be reserved for patients with ASA 3 or higher
statements.explanation: Not limited to ASA 3+; used per clinical need.

statements.text: the femoral artery should be avoided due to a high rate of complications
statements.explanation: Femoral arterial cannulation is acceptable when radial is difficult.

statements.text: the Allen test reliably identifies patients at high risk of ischemic complications from radial artery catheterization
statements.explanation: Allen test has poor predictive value for ischaemia.

statements.text: in the presence of atrial fibrillation, it allows better monitoring of arterial pressure
statements.explanation: In AF, oscillometric is hard but invasive measurement is unchanged.

stem: During a posterior fossa craniotomy, inadvertent surgical stimulation of the tenth cranial nerve will produce:
explanation: Posterior fossa surgery: cranial nerve monitoring — vagus stimulation → bradycardia; trigeminal → bradycardia (oculocardiac); accessory → shoulder movement.

statements.text: bradycardia
statements.explanation: CN X (vagus) stimulation causes bradycardia.

statements.text: nystagmus
statements.explanation: Nystagmus is CN VIII or III/IV/VI involvement.

statements.text: shoulder jerk or reflex movement
statements.explanation: Shoulder jerk is CN XI (accessory).

statements.text: facial spasms
statements.explanation: Facial spasms are CN VII.

statements.text: lower jaw spasms
statements.explanation: Jaw movement is CN V motor.

stem: Regarding sickle cell anemia:
explanation: Sickle cell anaemia: β-globin Glu6Val mutation → HbS polymerisation in deoxy state → vaso-occlusion; preoperative transfusion may reduce risk.

statements.text: it is characterized by high levels of hemoglobin
statements.explanation: Sickle cell anaemia has LOW (not high) HbF in most cases; HbS dominates.

statements.text: it may require preoperative transfusion
statements.explanation: Preoperative transfusion may be required to dilute HbS.

statements.text: it results from a point mutation in the beta-globin gene
statements.explanation: Point mutation in beta-globin (glu→val at position 6).

statements.text: it is diagnosed by genetic analysis
statements.explanation: Genetic analysis confirms diagnosis; haemoglobin electrophoresis usually first.

statements.text: symptoms include vaso-occlusive events that can be extremely painful
statements.explanation: Vaso-occlusive crises are extremely painful.

stem: In ultrasound-guided regional anesthesia, the visibility of the needle tip is determined by:
explanation: Ultrasound needle visibility: keep needle parallel to probe (low angle), jiggle for movement detection, use echogenic needles, control depth.

statements.text: the acoustic properties of the tissues
statements.explanation: Tissue echogenicity affects needle visibility.

statements.text: the needle diameter
statements.explanation: Needle diameter has minor effect on visibility.

statements.text: needle movement
statements.explanation: Movement (jiggling) enhances visibility — useful technique.

statements.text: the angle of needle insertion
statements.explanation: Insertion angle markedly affects visibility (steeper = worse).

statements.text: the depth of needle insertion
statements.explanation: Greater depth reduces visibility (deeper needle harder to see).

stem: In late-onset ventilator-associated pneumonia, the recommended initial empirical antibiotic therapy includes:
explanation: Late VAP empirical cover targets multi-resistant organisms: anti-pseudomonal β-lactam + anti-MRSA + sometimes aminoglycoside or quinolone.

statements.text: ceftriaxone when Pseudomonas aeruginosa is suspected
statements.explanation: Pseudomonas: needs antipseudomonal (piperacillin-tazobactam or carbapenem), not ceftriaxone alone.

statements.text: ceftazidime when Legionella pneumoniae is suspected
statements.explanation: Legionella: macrolide/fluoroquinolone first-line; ceftazidime doesn’t cover.

statements.text: ampicillin/sulbactam when Acinetobacter positive for extended-spectrum beta-lactamase is suspected
statements.explanation: ESBL Acinetobacter: covered by carbapenems or ampicillin/sulbactam (for sulbactam intrinsic activity).

statements.text: vancomycin when Streptococcus pneumoniae is suspected
statements.explanation: S. pneumoniae: vancomycin only if penicillin-resistant; first-line is amoxicillin/penicillin.

statements.text: meropenem when methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus is suspected
statements.explanation: MRSA: vancomycin or linezolid first-line; meropenem doesn’t cover MRSA. Statement is FALSE actually.

stem: Pneumoperitoneum decreases:
explanation: Laparoscopic pneumoperitoneum: ↑ETCO2 and ICP; ↓CO, compliance, GFR; tolerable with adjusted ventilation.

statements.text: end-tidal CO2
statements.explanation: Pneumoperitoneum INCREASES end-tidal CO2 (CO2 absorption).

statements.text: intracranial pressure
statements.explanation: Pneumoperitoneum can INCREASE ICP from raised intra-abdominal pressure.

statements.text: cardiac output
statements.explanation: Pneumoperitoneum reduces venous return → ↓CO.

statements.text: compliance
statements.explanation: Pneumoperitoneum reduces respiratory compliance (diaphragm splinted).

statements.text: glomerular filtration rate
statements.explanation: Pneumoperitoneum reduces renal perfusion → ↓GFR.

stem: Anesthesia and surgery complicate the treatment of patients with diabetes mellitus because:
explanation: Perioperative diabetes: stress hormones → hyperglycaemia; GA masks hypoglycaemia; titrate insulin/glucose; tight monitoring.

statements.text: inhalational anesthetics prolong the half-life of insulin
statements.explanation: Inhalational agents don’t prolong insulin half-life.

statements.text: signs of hypoglycemia are masked
statements.explanation: GA masks hypoglycaemia signs (sweating, tremor, tachycardia).

statements.text: cortisol release antagonizes the effects of insulin
statements.explanation: Cortisol release (stress response) antagonises insulin.

statements.text: inhibition of potassium and glucose co-transport systems leads to hyperkalemia
statements.explanation: Insulin/glucose co-transport doesn’t describe hyperkalaemia mechanism accurately.

statements.text: hyperglycemia accompanies sympathetic stimulation
statements.explanation: Sympathetic stimulation → hyperglycaemia via glucagon and catecholamines.

stem: During labor, epidural anesthesia with bupivacaine:
explanation: Labour epidural: indicated in pre-eclampsia, sickle cell, cardiac disease; reduces stress response; modern data show no/minor increase in instrumental delivery.

statements.text: is contraindicated in pregnant women with diabetic neuropathy
statements.explanation: Diabetic neuropathy is not an absolute contraindication.

statements.text: frequently masks uterine rupture
statements.explanation: Epidural may mask abdominal pain of uterine rupture but is not the dominant masking factor (decelerations remain).

statements.text: is contraindicated in pre-eclampsia
statements.explanation: Pre-eclampsia is an INDICATION (not contraindication) for epidural.

statements.text: reduces the incidence of instrumental delivery
statements.explanation: Epidural reduces instrumental delivery and CS rate per modern Cochrane reviews — mixed evidence; classical answer is REDUCES (some texts say increases). Conservative TRUE per modern evidence.

statements.text: is contraindicated in pregnant women with sickle cell disease
statements.explanation: Sickle cell disease is not contraindication; epidural may help by reducing crisis risk.
